behavior_summary: This script implements a single 'U8P_FEBARSEW::look' (examine) handler for the entity: when the player examines the object, the game displays the short text "iron bars " and then the process suspends/ends.
There is no branching, menu interaction, flag reads/writes, or external calls — the behavior is a one-line descriptive bark followed by termination of the U8P_FEBARSEW::look process.
The presence of two low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes in the decompiled output suggests minor low-level remnants (e.g., delimiters or a suspend op) but they do not change the observable behavior.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FEBARSEW::look executes as a process: output a single bark string "iron bars ", then suspend/terminate the U8P_FEBARSEW::look process.
No conditional branches, loops, or calls are present in the canonical control flow — it is linear and deterministic.
open_questions: The disassembly contains two 'Unknown' node samples ('{' and 'suspend') — are these artifacts of decompilation or should they map to a specific engine internal operation beyond the observable suspend/terminate?
The bark text includes a trailing space ('iron bars ') — is this intentional for concatenation in other contexts, or simply the stored string?
